title: dactylis glomerata subsp. woronowii
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. woronowii, also known as Woronow's cock's foot, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae, native to Central Asia and Eastern Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. woronowii include relieving congestion, reducing inflammation, and soothing irritated mucous membranes. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
